object_types.name: Customer
object_types.definition: A person or organization that places orders in the CRM system.

source_references.excerpt: A customer places an order.
Each customer has a unique customer ID in the CRM system.

object_types.name: Order
object_types.definition: A confirmed request by a customer for one or more products.

source_references.excerpt: A customer places an order.
Each order has an order number. That's the primary identifier.

object_types.name: Product
object_types.definition: A product that can be ordered by customers.

source_references.excerpt: An order contains one or more products.
By SKU -- a unique stock-keeping unit code.

object_types.name: OrderStatus
object_types.definition: The current status of an order in the fulfillment pipeline.

source_references.excerpt: The possible values are: pending, confirmed, shipped, and delivered.
every customer has a name. It's just a text field.
A customer places an order.
An order contains one or more products.
an order has a status
every customer has a name

inferred_constraints.description: Each Order is placed by at most one Customer.

source_references.excerpt: Every order belongs to exactly one customer

inferred_constraints.description: Every Order is placed by some Customer.

source_references.excerpt: Every order belongs to exactly one customer

inferred_constraints.description: Each Order has at most one OrderStatus.

source_references.excerpt: an order has a status
Every order must have a status

inferred_constraints.description: Each Customer has at most one Name.

source_references.excerpt: every customer has a name
every customer has a name

ambiguities.description: Stakeholders use 'customer' (CRM) and 'client' (billing) to refer to what appears to be the same concept, but billing only considers someone a client once they have paid. This may require separate entity types or a subtype relationship.

source_references.excerpt: I've heard some people call customers "clients" in the billing system.
they only consider someone a client once they've actually paid
